text: The most elegant simple tool is the wedge. It converts downward or horizontal force into outward force through pure geometry—a triangle of material with no moving parts, no springs, no calibration. A knife, axe, and doorstop all apply the same principle. What makes it elegant is that the physics scales identically whether used for detail work or splitting logs, the form perfectly mirrors its function, and there's nothing unnecessary. It's a minimal statement of an idea: this shape, this angle, this direction of force.
